allowed_claims: S2 follow-up is planned before S3.
Isaac Sim / Omniverse supports PBD particle fluid demos, but benchmark-ready static containment still requires fluid-safe colliders.
C2 is the first follow-up route because it is the closest failed S2 candidate.
blocked_claims: native LabUtopia beaker mesh is fluid-safe by default
particle contact reports can be used as score evidence

S2F0_BASELINE_FREEZE.goal: Freeze the existing S2 collider matrix as the baseline before any follow-up tuning.
S2F0_BASELINE_FREEZE.release_condition: S3 remains unreleased; baseline evidence is copied into this S2F plan manifest.

S2F1_C2_PROXY_SWEEP.goal: Repair the closest failed S2 candidate by sweeping segmented proxy geometry and contact offsets.
S2F1_C2_PROXY_SWEEP.release_condition: At least one C2A candidate satisfies PASS_SOURCE_HOLD under s2_no_outside_source_v2.

S2F2_VELOCITY_CONTACT_OFFSET.goal: Separate geometry leak from particle/contact parameter sensitivity.
S2F2_VELOCITY_CONTACT_OFFSET.release_condition: A candidate passes without non-physical max_velocity damping dependence.

S2F3_C3_SDF_SWEEP.goal: Test whether an open concave SDF tri-mesh can be a fluid-safe beaker collider.
S2F3_C3_SDF_SWEEP.release_condition: A C3A candidate passes static hold without SDF cooking, GPU, or perf failures.

S2F4_C4_NATIVE_MESH_ISOLATION.goal: Determine whether native LabUtopia beaker2 mesh can be salvaged or must be wrapped by a proxy collider.
S2F4_C4_NATIVE_MESH_ISOLATION.release_condition: Native-derived route passes or writes NATIVE_BEAKER_NOT_FLUID_SAFE_COLLIDER.

S2F5_PROMOTION_REVIEW.goal: Promote one or more non-negative-control candidates to S3 only after same-run readback and warning evidence.
S2F5_PROMOTION_REVIEW.release_condition: best_for_s3 is non-empty; otherwise s3_kinematic_pour_released remains false.

forum_experience: NVIDIA forum reports liquid particles passing through colliders can be sensitive to particle contact offset.
Forum/GitHub discussions indicate PBD particles are GPU-only and must use GPU-compatible collider approximations.
